Also, I'm on the hunt for the best French cuisine restaurant for lunch on Sunday. Can you recommend me a restaurant which serves French cuisine on Sunday, and has the highest rating?
Can you give me an estimate of the minimum cost for the trip? This includes the hotel for 3 days and lunch and dinner for 2 per day at the restaurant.
Looking forward to hearing your recommendations! Tell me the hotel name and the restaurant name. Thanks in advance.
Let me start by pulling up all the information we need — hotels in Paris, restaurants in Paris, and your user details. One moment!

content.content:
Here's a summary of my findings, Emma!

---

## 🏨 Hotel Recommendation: **Montmartre Suites**

Here's the breakdown of Paris hotels under $210/night:

| Hotel | Price/Night | Rating |
|---|---|---|
| **Montmartre Suites** | $110 – $200 | ⭐4.7 |
| Le Marais Boutique | $120 – $180 | ⭐4.2 |
| Good Night | $240+ | ❌ Over budget |
| Luxury Palace | $500+ | ❌ Over budget |

**Montmartre Suites** wins with a 4.7 rating and a minimum price of **$110/night**. Luxurious suites with stunning Montmartre views, impeccable cleanliness, and a perfect location for exploring the charming neighbourhood.

---

## 🍽️ Restaurant Recommendation: **Breizh Café**

Of the four French cuisine restaurants in Paris, **only Breizh Café is open on Sundays** (9 AM – 11 PM), so it's your pick by default! Here's how the French options compare:

| French Restaurant | Rating | Open Sunday? |
|---|---|---|
| Le Baratin | ⭐4.8 | ❌ |
| Bistrot Paul Bert | ⭐4.5 | ❌ |
| Chez L'Ami Jean | ⭐4.4 | ❌ |
| **Breizh Café** | ⭐3.9 | ✅ |

Known for the best crêpes in Paris (both sweet and savory), authentic Breton cider, and gluten-free buckwheat options. Price: **$60/person**.

---

## 💰 Minimum Trip Cost Estimate

| Item | Calculation | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| **Hotel** (3 nights) | $110 × 3 | **$330** |
| **Lunch** (2 people × 3 days) | $60 × 2 × 3 | **$360** |
| **Dinner** (2 people × 3 days) | $60 × 2 × 3 | **$360** |
| **Total** | | **$1,050** |

So you're looking at a minimum of around **$1,050** for the trip, assuming you stick to the lowest hotel rate and dine at Breizh Café for all lunches and dinners.

Would you like me to go ahead and book Montmartre Suites for May 10–13 and reserve a table at Breizh Café for Sunday lunch (May 11)?
